response.justification: The candidate is an experienced and versatile senior software developer with a strong web, full-stack, and open-source background, but the role requires deep specialization in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. His closest relevant experience (low-latency DLang services and brief C/C++ IDE work at National Instruments) is systems programming, not silicon-level performance architecture, and there is no evidence of deep-learning workload optimization or SIMD/MIMD architecture work. He meets the years-of-experience bar with a BS, but the domain mismatch at Principal level is disqualifying.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang, async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or hardware performance-analysis experience
No deep neural network training/inference optimization or AI accelerator background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, far from Principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware co-design experience
